blocks.text: DTC P0562: Charging device voltage low

The LIN-connected A.C. generator converts a portion of the engine output into electric power via the drive belt, receives instructions from the PGM-FI ECU via LIN on the voltage to be generated, and generates power accordingly The only connection terminals between the A.C. generator and the vehicle side are the +B terminal (output terminal) and LIN communication terminal (1P connector ), and the enclosure of the A.C. generator is the GND. The power supply for regulator operation is connected internally using the B terminal. 12.5V-14.5V, the target generator voltage of the A.C. generator, is indicated by the PGM-FI ECU via LIN. FI ECU judges it as a failure and stores DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: off
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed

blocks.text: When the FI MAIN RLY OUT terminal voltage of the PGM-FI ECU remains below 11.0V for 60 seconds or longer.

items.text: A.C. Generator stops generating power due to over-temperature (when P16E4 is stored at the same time)
A.C. +B terminal coupler on generator side disconnected
A.C. generator failure (broken field coil, broken stator coil, regulator failure)

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory without the PGM-FI warning light being turned on.
Temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
